initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> RED is now asking for 58 ZUP. This is still within my budget of 60 ZUP. I will counter with 60 ZUP, which is my maximum willingness to pay. This is my second proposal, so I want to try and close the deal quickly.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message> RED, I can go up to 60 ZUP for X. This is my final offer before I have to consider other options. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: Looks OK. All tags are present in the correct order and seem to contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Offering 60 ZUP is exactly at my maximum willingness to pay, so it maximizes my payoff if accepted.
3. Opponent plausibility: Potentially risky. RED initially asked for 50, then countered with 58. Jumping straight to 60 might be seen as a hard limit and could lead to rejection. A slightly lower increment (e.g., 59) might be more palatable.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason>, <player answer>, <newly proposed trade>, and <message> are all consistent with each other.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is my second proposal, leaving me with one remaining.
</critique>
